NVDA.reasoning:
CATALYST WINDOW: Hyperscaler AI capex disclosures (Alphabet, MSFT, AMZN, META earnings week of Apr 28, currently live) are the primary catalyst — each confirmed incremental GPU spend is a direct demand signal for NVDA as the ~81% AI chip market share leader. This catalyst is in-window NOW (Apr 30, 2026). Secondary catalyst: NVDA own earnings ~May 27 AMC (~18 trading days out, outside entry window but sets a hard exit deadline of ~May 17 per the 3-day pre-earnings blackout). Fed rate decision today (Apr 30) widely expected hold — low impact, already priced.

NET NEWS SENTIMENT: Positive. NVDA hit a 52-week high of $216.83 on Apr 28, up 27% in 21 trading days. Intel Q1 2026 blowout ($13.58B vs $12.42B est.) and AMD record data center revenue both confirm AI hardware demand acceleration. Blackwell Ultra leads market by two generations; Rubin architecture on deck for late 2026. LG humanoid/AI partnership (Apr 29) expands customer base. Negatives: H20 China export ban = $4.5B Q1 charge (already absorbed, priced in); Alphabet TPU long-term competitive risk (not near-term); OpenAI revenue miss (Apr 28) creates mild uncertainty but hyperscaler capex is a separate dynamic.

INSIDER ACTIVITY: Quiet in current 30-day window (no recent trades in data). Wiki notes consistent insider selling trend (CFO and board member sold at $171-177 in March 2026 on likely 10b5-1 plans — well below current $209 price). Not an acute near-term signal; treat as background bearish lean.

TECHNICAL SETUP QUALITY: Constructive. All daily EMAs aligned uptrend. Price at $209.25 has pulled back ~3.5% from the $216.83 high on declining volume (relative volume 0.84 vs 20-day avg), suggesting a normal post-breakout consolidation rather than distribution. Daily ADX at 54.67 confirms strong trend. Daily RSI-14 at 74.76 / RSI-21 at 81.52 are elevated (overbought caution flag), but given the 26.69% ROC over 21 days and catalyst momentum this is not disqualifying. MACD histogram positive (+1.80) and expanding. Hourly RSI-21 at 51.94 — neutral/balanced intraday, not exhausted. Hourly MACD slightly negative (-0.59) but within normal consolidation range. Hourly volume bias 10d is down and OBV down on hourly — some informed distribution concern, but daily OBV trend remains up with no price divergence. Key entry support: $208.20 (volume-confirmed hourly pivot, 15 tests). Pre-catalyst daily support: $179.17 (15 tests). Nearest resistance: $214.73 (hourly), then $216.83 (recent high). Fib ext targets: $221.89 (1.272) and $228.34 (1.618) from the hourly swing.
